prompt: Run spring_clean → rebug pipeline on a synthetic test repo with 28+ planted issues across Security, Correctness, Performance, and Error Handling.

assertions.text: Spring_clean detects the SQL injection in search_tasks (SEC-3)
Spring_clean detects hardcoded JWT secret key (SEC-2)
Spring_clean detects pickle.loads on untrusted data (SEC-5)
Spring_clean detects wildcard CORS with credentials (SEC-6)
Spring_clean detects division by zero in compute_task_stats (COR-2)
Spring_clean detects N+1 query pattern in get_user_tasks (PERF-1)
Spring_clean detects import typo 'taks' in test file (COR-7)
Spring_clean detects arbitrary SQL execution endpoint (SEC-4)
Spring_clean detects at least 20 of the 28+ planted issues (>70% recall)
Spring_clean has no more than 2 false positives (issues that don't exist)
Rebug produces a verdict for every finding from spring_clean
Rebug confirms the genuinely planted issues rather than disputing them
Rebug uses the verdict taxonomy (Confirmed/Disputed/Partially valid/etc.)
